source_book: The 4 Disciplines of Execution (2nd ed., 2021) — Chris McChesney, Sean Covey, Jim Huling, Scott Thele, Beverly Walker
_comment: Topic-router for ambiguous-scope D1 WIG-formulation queries. Tests cover EN/JP/zh-TW. Activation criterion = ambiguous on (scope, verb) per description.

test_cases.prompt: Help me with my WIG. I don't know where to start.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d1-wig-formulation. 'My WIG' is ambiguous — could mean solo personal WIG, or a member's view of the team WIG, or a leader thinking 'my (team's) WIG'. Router asks ONE Socratic question on (scope, verb) then routes.
test_cases.notes: positive: matches EN signal 'Help me with my WIG' literally cited in description

test_cases.prompt: WIG をどう設定すればいいですか？
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d1-wig-formulation. JP query 'WIG をどう選ぶ / 作り方' without actor or verb. Router disambiguates scope (solo / team-leader / member) and verb (define / select / comprehend).
test_cases.notes: positive: matches JP signal 「WIG をどう選ぶ」「WIG の作り方」 in description

test_cases.prompt: 幫我搞 WIG，目標太多不知道怎麼挑。
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d1-wig-formulation. zh-TW query with no scope/verb marker. Router should ask the 2-part question (whose WIG / which verb) then route.
test_cases.notes: positive: matches zh-TW signal 「幫我搞 WIG」「目標太多怎麼挑」 in description

test_cases.prompt: How do I pick the right goal for the next quarter? Multiple things on my plate.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d1-wig-formulation. 'Pick the right goal' is described as a trigger phrasing. No actor named (could be solo or leader). Router disambiguates.
test_cases.notes: positive: description lists 'Pick the right goal' as EN trigger phrasing; scope unclear

test_cases.prompt: Help me write my personal WIG: From 78kg to 70kg by Dec 31.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate 4dx-d1-wig-formulation. Scope (personal) and verb (define) both explicit + canonical From-X-to-Y-by-When form already supplied. Fire 4dx-d1-personal-wig-defining directly.
test_cases.notes: lure: explicit personal+define — description excludes scope-explicit cases

test_cases.prompt: I have my WIG defined. What lead measures should I track?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate 4dx-d1-wig-formulation. Topic is wrong — query is D2 lead-measure discovery, not D1 WIG formulation. Route to 4dx-d2-personal-lead-measure-discovery.
test_cases.notes: lure: wrong topic — surface 'WIG' word present but scope is downstream (D2)

test_cases.prompt: How do I cascade my org's Primary WIG down to 5 sub-teams?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate 4dx-d1-wig-formulation. Description explicitly excludes cascade queries — different verb (translate, not select) and different skill: 4dx-d1-team-wig-cascade.
test_cases.notes: lure: cascade is a sibling D1 topic explicitly excluded by description ('cascading downward to subordinate teams')

test_cases.prompt: 我老闆要我幫忙想 team 的 WIG，可是最後決定權在他。我要怎麼準備？
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d1-wig-formulation. Edge case: user is a member contributing to leader's selection. Not pure 'comprehend an inherited WIG' (no WIG yet) and not 'select' (member lacks authority). Router should surface this hybrid and likely route to member-team-wig-comprehension framing (member's perspective on candidate WIGs) OR suggest user pass team-primary-wig-selection content to the leader.
test_cases.notes: boundary: member-contributing-to-selection — sits between team-select (leader's verb) and member-comprehend (post-decision); router's disambiguation question is exactly the right tool

self_evaluation_note: All 8 cases reviewed against description-only activation. 4 should_trigger genuinely scope-ambiguous; 3 should_not_trigger lures (scope+verb explicit / wrong topic D2 / wrong topic cascade) correctly excluded. Edge case forces router to hold the hybrid case open via Socratic disambiguation rather than silently picking a path.
